channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Switchable 2D–3D display through a metasurface lenticular lens
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10318-9

A full-colour 2D–3D switchable light-field display powered by a metasurface lenticular lens is proposed that is a promising solution for next-generation display technologies in both consumer electronics and commercial applications.
item.dc:title: Switchable 2D–3D display through a metasurface lenticular lens
item.dc:creator: Seokil Moon
Joohoon Kim
Youngjin Jo
Juwon Seo
Kyungtae Kim
Seokwoo Kim
Chang-Kun Lee
Junsuk Rho

item.title: Electronic origin of reorganization energy in interfacial electron transfer
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10311-2

By tuning graphene’s electronic density of states, the study shows electrode electronic structure—not just the electrolyte—dominates reorganization energy and thus controls outer-sphere electron-transfer rates at solid–liquid interfaces.
item.dc:title: Electronic origin of reorganization energy in interfacial electron transfer
item.dc:creator: Sonal Maroo
Leonardo Coello Escalante
Yizhe Wang
Matthew P. Erodici
Jonathon N. Nessralla
Ayana Tabo
Takashi Taniguchi
Kenji Watanabe
Ke Xu
David T. Limmer
D. Kwabena Bediako

item.title: Printable meta-assemblies enable synergetic colouration
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10408-8

A printable meta-assembly comprising polystyrene embedded in a polydimethylsiloxane matrix used in roll-to-roll additive nanoprinting is described for the fabrication of highly tunable optical architectures that can span seven orders of magnitude in length.
item.dc:title: Printable meta-assemblies enable synergetic colouration
item.dc:creator: Kaixuan Li
Jianfeng Chen
Huizeng Li
An Li
Xiaoyu Hou

Zhimei Jia
Mingzhu Li
Joel K. W. Yang
Cheng-Wei Qiu
Yanlin Song

item.title: Symmetry classification of magnetic orders using oriented spin space groups
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10401-1

An oriented spin space group description is proposed that unifies the spin space group and magnetic space group frameworks, enabling a comprehensive symmetry classification of the magnetic materials on a large scale and identifying spin–orbit magnetism as a distinct phase.
item.dc:title: Symmetry classification of magnetic orders using oriented spin space groups
item.dc:creator: Yuntian Liu
Xiaobing Chen
Yutong Yu
Jesús Etxebarria
J. Manuel Perez-Mato
Qihang Liu

item.title: Mechanically driven Li dendrite penetration in garnet solid electrolyte
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10415-9

Cryogenic electron microscopy images and micromechanical fracture modelling of mechanically soft lithium dendrites fracturing hard ceramic electrolytes suggest the mechanisms driving the phenomenon as well as design implications for solid-state lithium metal batteries.
item.dc:title: Mechanically driven Li dendrite penetration in garnet solid electrolyte
item.dc:creator: Yuwei Zhang
Soroush Motahari
Eric V. Woods
Stefan Zaefferer
Peter Schweizer
Zhiyuan Zhang
Yuqi Liu
Baptiste Gault

Christina Scheu
Yug Joshi
Siyuan Zhang
Chuanlai Liu
Gerhard Dehm

item.title: Phonon Hall viscosity and the intrinsic thermal Hall effect of α-RuCl<sub>3</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10420-y

Ultrasonic measurements show phonon Hall viscosity drives an intrinsic thermal Hall effect in α-RuCl3, resolving its origin.
item.dc:title: Phonon Hall viscosity and the intrinsic thermal Hall effect of α-RuCl<sub>3</sub>
item.dc:creator: Avi Shragai
Ezekiel Horsley
Subin Kim
Young-June Kim
B. J. Ramshaw

item.title: Hybrid calculation of hadronic vacuum polarization in muon g − 2 to 0.48%
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10449-z

A new calculation for the anomalous magnetic moment of the muon using a hybrid approach combining experimental and lattice data in different energy ranges represents a validation of the standard model to 11 digits.
item.dc:title: Hybrid calculation of hadronic vacuum polarization in muon g − 2 to 0.48%
item.dc:creator: A. Boccaletti
Sz. Borsanyi
A. Cotellucci

Z. Zhang

item.title: Ceramic-like strength and metallic toughness in a bulk metallic glass
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10430-w

Re–Co–Ta–B bulk metallic glasses have high strength, fracture toughness and thermal stability, combining traditionally exclusive properties of metals and ceramics.
item.dc:title: Ceramic-like strength and metallic toughness in a bulk metallic glass
item.dc:creator: Zhengqing Cai
Shidong Feng
Zhen-Qiang Song
Yanhui Liu
Bo Xu
Zijing Li
Yanhui Zhang
Yingdan Liu
Ruilin Zheng
Xinyu Zhang
Weihua Wang
Li-Min Wang
Ri-Ping Liu

item.title: Glasses-free display switches between 2D and 3D
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01148-w

A nanostructured ‘metasurface’ lens enables 3D images to be viewed without specialized glasses and can switch to 2D operation.
item.dc:title: Glasses-free display switches between 2D and 3D
item.dc:creator: Xiao Wei Sun
Zhongtao Tian
